step_by_step_thinking:
The lymphatic drainage of structures around the mouth follows specific patterns. Lower lip areas, particularly those closer to the middle region, primarily drain into submental nodes due to their anatomical proximity.

Answer: B To determine where lymph from the lower lip (specifically its mid-region) drains, it's important to understand regional anatomy. The submental nodes lie just below the chin and receive draining lymphatics from this area of the face including parts of the lower lip. Therefore,
